---
title: "Validate the OCP Cluster"
format: html
---

Run these checks before deploying Concert Operate. They catch environment blockers early
and avoid a failed or incomplete installation. At the end of this step you will have a
confirmed go / no-go for the cluster.

These checks apply to a Concert Operate v5.1.0 production base deployment on a
self-hosted OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) cluster. Hardware requirements are sourced
from the IBM Concert Operate v5.1.0 documentation (Table 4: Concert Operate + OpenShift,
new cluster, production base, without Hybrid storage).

## Prerequisites

- `oc` CLI installed and authenticated (`oc login` confirmed)
- `aiopsctl` v5.1.0 installed (required for storage benchmark and precheck; see Install section below)
- Cluster-admin or equivalent read access to inspect nodes, storage, and network resources
- Namespace creation rights (or a pre-created target namespace)
- Contact established with the platform / storage team for items that cannot be confirmed
  from the CLI alone (StorageClass filesystem type, NetworkPolicy defaults)

### Install `aiopsctl`

Download the binary for your platform and put it on your PATH. Use the version-pinned URL
to ensure you have v5.1.0.

```bash
# Replace the filename with your platform:
# aiopsctl-linux_amd64.tar.gz  |  aiopsctl-linux_arm64.tar.gz
# aiopsctl-darwin_amd64.tar.gz |  aiopsctl-darwin_arm64.tar.gz
# Windows: run from a WSL terminal using the linux_amd64 binary

AIOPSCTL_TAR=aiopsctl-linux_amd64.tar.gz
curl -LO "https://github.com/IBM/aiopsctl/releases/download/v5.1.0/${AIOPSCTL_TAR}"
tar xf "${AIOPSCTL_TAR}"
mv aiopsctl /usr/local/bin/aiopsctl
aiopsctl version   # expected: v5.1.0
```

---

## 1. OCP Version and Architecture

Concert Operate v5.1.0 supports **OCP v4.16 through v4.21** on **x86\_64 only**. ARM and
ppc64le architectures are not supported.

```bash
oc version
```

Expected output (example):

```
Client Version: 4.18.x
Server Version: 4.18.x
Kubernetes Version: v1.31.x
```

For architecture, check the `KERNEL-VERSION` column of `oc get nodes -o wide`. On x86\_64
nodes the kernel version ends in `.x86_64`:

```bash
oc get nodes -o wide
```

Expected `KERNEL-VERSION` value (example):

```
5.14.0-427.130.1.el9_4.x86_64
```

All nodes must show `.x86_64` in their kernel version. Any other suffix (`.aarch64`,
`.s390x`) means the architecture requirement is not met.

| Check | Command | What to look for |
|---|---|---|
| OCP version in range | `oc version` | Server Version: 4.16.x – 4.21.x |
| Node architecture | `oc get nodes -o wide` | All nodes: `KERNEL-VERSION` ends in `.x86_64` |

---

## 2. Compute: Worker Nodes

The production base profile requires a minimum of **6 worker nodes**. Per-node minimums are
**16 vCPU** and **20 GB RAM**. The cluster as a whole must satisfy the aggregate totals:
158 vCPU and 384 GB RAM across all workers. If nodes are provisioned below the per-node
minimums, additional nodes are required to reach those totals.

**Count worker nodes:**

```bash
oc get nodes --selector='node-role.kubernetes.io/worker'
```

**Capacity and Allocatable for all workers at once:**

`oc describe node` is verbose. Use `jsonpath` to pull just the numbers you need across all
worker nodes in one pass:

```bash
for node in $(oc get nodes --selector='node-role.kubernetes.io/worker' -o name); do
  echo "--- ${node} ---"
  oc get ${node} -o jsonpath=\
'CPU capacity: {.status.capacity.cpu}{"\n"}'\
'CPU allocatable: {.status.allocatable.cpu}{"\n"}'\
'Memory capacity (GiB): {.status.capacity.memory}{"\n"}'\
'Memory allocatable (GiB): {.status.allocatable.memory}{"\n"}'\
'Ephemeral storage capacity (GiB): {.status.capacity.ephemeral-storage}{"\n"}'\
'Ephemeral storage allocatable (GiB): {.status.allocatable.ephemeral-storage}{"\n"}'
done
```

Memory and storage values are returned in `Ki` (kibibytes). Divide by 1,048,576 to convert
to GiB, or use `oc adm top nodes` for a live utilization view in human-readable units:

```bash
oc adm top nodes --selector='node-role.kubernetes.io/worker'
```

**Aggregate capacity across all nodes (one line per node):**

```bash
oc get nodes -o=custom-columns=\
'NAME:.metadata.name,'\
'CPU:.status.capacity.cpu,'\
'MEMORY_Ki:.status.capacity.memory,'\
'STORAGE_Ki:.status.capacity.ephemeral-storage'
```

Raw Ki values are expected here. For a node showing `131856920Ki` memory, that is
approximately 125 GiB (131,856,920 / 1,048,576). Multiply the per-node GiB by the worker
count to confirm the cluster total meets 384 GB.

Allocatable is capacity minus OS and kubelet overhead. It is the figure that matters for
scheduling.

| Check | Per-node minimum | Aggregate target |
|---|---|---|
| Worker node count | n/a | ≥ 6 nodes |
| vCPU per worker | ≥ 16 vCPU | 158 vCPU total |
| RAM per worker | ≥ 20 GB | 384 GB total |
| Ephemeral storage per worker | ≥ 120 GB | n/a |
| Control plane nodes | n/a | 3 nodes |

---

## 3. Storage: StorageClass

Storage class configuration **cannot be changed after installation**. Get this right before
proceeding.

### 3a. Identify the StorageClass to use

Start by listing all available StorageClasses:

```bash
oc get sc
```

Example output:

```
NAME                          PROVISIONER                    RECLAIMPOLICY   VOLUMEBINDINGMODE      ALLOWVOLUMEEXPANSION
ocs-storagecluster-ceph-rbd   openshift-storage.rbd.csi...   Delete          Immediate              true
ocs-storagecluster-cephfs     openshift-storage.cephfs...    Delete          Immediate              true
```

The StorageClass for Concert Operate must be a **block (RWO)** class backed by a CSI driver.
Look for classes provisioned by a block CSI driver (`rbd`, `nutanix`, `portworx`, `gp3`,
etc.). CephFS and NFS classes provision filesystem (RWX) volumes; those are not the right
choice for the primary Concert Operate storage class.

If multiple block classes exist, confirm which one is appropriate with your platform team.
Note the name; you will use it in the installation CR and in the checks below. Replace
`<storage-class-name>` with that name throughout this section.

The `oc get sc` output already shows `ALLOWVOLUMEEXPANSION` and `VOLUMEBINDINGMODE` for all
classes at a glance. Use `oc get sc <storage-class-name> -o yaml` for full detail on a
specific class.

### 3b. Verify filesystem type is XFS

MinIO requires XFS. An ext4 filesystem will exhaust inodes even when PVC space is available,
leaving the filesystem read-only with no recovery path.

```bash
oc get sc <storage-class-name> -o yaml | grep -E 'fsType|csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype'
```

Look for `fsType: xfs` or `csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: xfs` in the parameters block. If the
field is missing or set to `ext4`, the StorageClass must be reconfigured or a new
XFS-configured class must be requested from the storage team before installation.

> **Note:** Some CSI drivers (e.g. Nutanix CSI) default to ext4. Confirm explicitly with
> the platform team. Do not assume the default is XFS.

### 3c. Verify `allowVolumeExpansion: true`

Required for runtime PVC resizing without downtime:

```bash
oc get sc <storage-class-name> -o yaml | grep allowVolumeExpansion
```

Must return `allowVolumeExpansion: true`. If it returns `false` or is absent, ask the
platform team to enable it on the StorageClass before installation.

### 3d. Check `volumeBindingMode`

```bash
oc get sc <storage-class-name> -o yaml | grep volumeBindingMode
```

Either `Immediate` or `WaitForFirstConsumer` is acceptable. Confirm it is compatible with
your cluster topology (single-zone vs. multi-zone).

### 3e. PVC smoke test

Create a small test PVC to confirm the StorageClass provisions and binds successfully.
Replace `<storage-class-name>` with the confirmed class name.

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: concert-storage-smoke-test
  namespace: default
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  storageClassName: <storage-class-name>
  volumeMode: Filesystem
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi
```

```bash
oc apply -f smoke-test-pvc.yaml
oc get pvc concert-storage-smoke-test   # expect: Bound within ~60 seconds
oc delete pvc concert-storage-smoke-test
```

If the PVC stays in `Pending`, check events for provisioning errors:

```bash
oc describe pvc concert-storage-smoke-test
```

---

## 4. Storage: Capacity and Performance

### 4a. Total PVC capacity

The production profile requires **2,627 Gi** of usable PVC capacity. Check existing PV
availability and confirm no pre-existing large claims will crowd out the installation:

```bash
oc get pv
oc get pvc -A   # check all namespaces for existing large claims
```

### 4b. IOPS: benchmark with `aiopsctl`

Run the built-in storage benchmark against your block StorageClass. The namespace must
already exist; create it first if needed:

```bash
oc new-project <TARGET_NAMESPACE>
```

Then run the benchmark. It takes approximately 8 minutes and will prompt for confirmation
before adding load to the cluster.

```bash
aiopsctl benchmark storage \
  --namespace=<TARGET_NAMESPACE> \
  --storage-class=<storage-class-name>
```

If storage performance varies significantly by node (e.g. nodes have different underlying
disk tiers), target a specific node:

```bash
aiopsctl benchmark storage \
  --namespace=<TARGET_NAMESPACE> \
  --storage-class=<storage-class-name> \
  --node=<node-name>
```

Example output:

```
Benchmark Results:
Measurement                 Read                           Write
Sequential IOPS             10831.66      ✔ (> 5000 )      6665.42       ✔ (> 5000 )
Sequential Bandwidth        40.87 Mi/sec  ✔ (> 20 Mi/sec)  26.08 Mi/sec  ✔ (> 20 Mi/sec)
Average-Sequential Latency  626.56 usec   ✖ (< 500 usec)   1133.56 usec  ✖ (< 1000 usec)
Mixed-Sequential IOPS       2260.35                        6778.59
```

Minimum passing thresholds:

| Metric | Read | Write |
|---|---|---|
| Minimum sequential IOPS | 5,000 | 5,000 |
| Minimum sequential bandwidth | 20 MiB/s | 20 MiB/s |
| Maximum average sequential latency | 500 µsec | 1,000 µsec |

IOPS and bandwidth failures are hard blockers. **Latency failures are a warning.** Network-attached storage often exceeds the latency thresholds
under normal conditions while still being suitable for Concert Operate in practice. If
latency fails, note it and raise it with the storage team, but do not halt validation
solely on latency. IOPS and bandwidth must pass.

These thresholds do not apply to native public cloud storage (Amazon EBS, IBM Cloud Block
Storage).

---

## 5. Network

### 5a. Cluster egress to the private container registry

Confirm the cluster can reach your private registry from within the cluster nodes, not just
from a workstation. Use a debug pod on a worker node:

```bash
# Option 1: curl from the node host
oc debug node/<worker-node-name> -- chroot /host \
  curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://<REGISTRY_HOST>

# Expected: 200 or 401 (auth challenge means network is reachable)

# Option 2: pull a known image via crictl
oc debug node/<worker-node-name> -- chroot /host \
  crictl pull <REGISTRY_HOST>/cpopen/<any-image>
```

Replace `<REGISTRY_HOST>` with your private registry hostname (Artifactory or equivalent).

The following IBM image source paths must be reachable through the registry proxy:

- `icr.io/cpopen`
- `icr.io/cpopen/cpfs`
- `cp.icr.io/cp`
- `cp.icr.io/cp/cp4waiops`

### 5b. NetworkPolicy defaults

Check whether the cluster applies default-deny NetworkPolicies to new namespaces:

```bash
oc get networkpolicy -A
```

If default-deny policies are in place, add explicit allow rules for the Concert Operate
namespace before installation. Check also for any proxy configuration that may need
`noProxy` entries for the registry hostname or the Concert Operate namespace:

```bash
oc get proxy cluster -o yaml
```

Confirm with the platform team before proceeding if either applies.

---

## 6. Namespace and RBAC

### 6a. Create the target namespace

If you have not already created the namespace for the storage benchmark above, create it now:

```bash
oc new-project <TARGET_NAMESPACE>
```

If the namespace was pre-created by the platform team, verify you can access it:

```bash
oc get namespace <TARGET_NAMESPACE>
oc project <TARGET_NAMESPACE>
```

### 6b. Confirm your user has sufficient rights

Concert Operate installation requires cluster-admin or an equivalent role that can create
CRDs and cluster-scoped resources. Check your current user:

```bash
oc auth can-i '*' '*'
```

If this returns `yes`, you have cluster-admin. If it returns `no`, check what cluster roles
are bound to your user:

```bash
oc get clusterrolebindings -o wide | grep $(oc whoami)
```

The install role must cover CRD creation, OperatorGroup and Subscription creation, and
namespace-scoped resource management. If the role is unclear, confirm with the platform
team before starting the installation.

---

## 7. Run the `aiopsctl` Precheck

Run the built-in precheck to validate OCP version, hardware, and storage in one pass.
At this stage, before cert-manager is installed, one check is expected to fail.

```bash
aiopsctl server precheck -n <TARGET_NAMESPACE>
```

Expected results on a fresh cluster:

| Check | Expected result | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Meets OCP Version Requirement | Passed | Must pass |
| Meets Hardware Requirements | Passed | Must pass |
| No Storage Issues | Passed | Must pass |
| Certificates Valid | Passed | Must pass |
| Cert Mgr Operator Exists | **Failed** | Expected at this stage; cert-manager is installed during the install step |

If any check other than `Cert Mgr Operator Exists` fails, resolve it before proceeding to
installation. A `Cert Mgr Operator Exists` failure here is normal and does not block you.

---

## What to do with your results

At this point you have run all pre-installation checks. Two types of findings are possible.

**Hard blockers (resolve before installing):**

- OCP version outside v4.16–v4.21
- Non-x86\_64 architecture on any node
- Worker node count or aggregate vCPU / RAM below minimums
- StorageClass not XFS, `allowVolumeExpansion: false`, or PVC smoke test fails
- Storage benchmark IOPS or bandwidth below threshold
- Registry unreachable from cluster nodes
- Current user lacks sufficient RBAC to install
- Any precheck result other than `Cert Mgr Operator Exists` fails

**Expected at this stage (not blockers):**

- `Cert Mgr Operator Exists` fails in the precheck (cert-manager gets installed during the install step)
- Latency benchmark warnings from network-attached storage (note them; raise with storage team if above 2x the threshold)

When all hard blockers are resolved, proceed to [Install Concert Operate](install.qmd).
